event_text: Brian Boru is killed after the Battle of Clontarf.
Anglo-Norman forces arrive in Ireland, beginning a new phase of conquest and settlement.
Henry II lands in Ireland and asserts English royal authority.
King John campaigns in Ireland and strengthens royal administration.
The first Irish parliament of the Lordship of Ireland is established.
Edward Bruce lands in Ireland, beginning a campaign against English power.
The Black Death reaches Ireland.
The Statutes of Kilkenny try to separate English settlers from Gaelic Irish society.
Poynings Law increases English control over the Irish parliament.
Silken Thomas publicly renounces allegiance to Henry VIII, beginning the Kildare rebellion.
Henry VIII is declared King of Ireland.
The plantation of Laois and Offaly begins under Mary I.
The First Desmond Rebellion begins in Munster.
The Second Desmond Rebellion begins and later ends with major confiscations in Munster.
The Nine Years War begins between Gaelic Irish lords and English government forces.
Irish and Spanish forces are defeated at the Battle of Kinsale.
The Flight of the Earls removes key Gaelic leaders from Ireland.
The Plantation of Ulster begins.
The Irish Rebellion of 1641 begins.
Cromwell’s campaign in Ireland leads to widespread destruction and land confiscation.
The Act of Settlement redistributes land after the wars of the seventeenth century.
The Williamite War in Ireland begins after James II lands at Kinsale.
William III defeats James II at the Battle of the Boyne.
The Treaty of Limerick ends the Williamite War in Ireland.
Penal laws begin restricting Catholic civil and political rights.
Jonathan Swift publishes A Modest Proposal.
The Great Frost and famine cause severe hardship across Ireland.
Grattan’s Parliament gains legislative independence for Ireland.
The Society of United Irishmen is founded in Belfast.
The Irish Rebellion of 1798 is suppressed.
The Acts of Union create the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland.
Robert Emmet leads a failed rebellion in Dublin.
Daniel O’Connell founds the Catholic Association.
Catholic Emancipation allows Catholics to sit in parliament.
The Tithe War protests compulsory payments to the Church of Ireland.
Potato blight is reported in Ireland, beginning the Great Famine period.
The Young Ireland rebellion takes place during famine and political unrest.
The Irish Republican Brotherhood is founded.
The Fenian Rising is suppressed.
The Home Government Association begins organised Home Rule campaigning.
The Irish National Land League is founded.
The Phoenix Park murders shock Irish and British politics.
The first Home Rule Bill is defeated in the House of Commons.
The second Home Rule Bill passes the Commons but is rejected by the Lords.
Sinn Féin is founded.
The third Home Rule Bill triggers unionist resistance in Ulster.
The Dublin Lock-out becomes a major labour conflict.
The First World War affects Ireland through enlistment, politics and economic change.
The Easter Rising is launched in Dublin and other locations.
Sinn Féin wins most Irish seats in the general election.
The Irish War of Independence is fought.
The Government of Ireland Act partitions the island into Northern Ireland and Southern Ireland.
The Anglo-Irish Treaty is signed.
The Irish Free State is established.
The Irish Civil War is fought over the Anglo-Irish Treaty.
Éamon de Valera becomes head of government.
A new constitution creates the state of Ireland, Éire.
Ireland declares a state of emergency and remains neutral during the Second World War.
Ireland becomes a republic and leaves the British Commonwealth.
Ireland joins the United Nations.
The Programme for Economic Expansion begins a shift in economic policy.
Violence in Northern Ireland marks the beginning of the Troubles.
Ireland joins the European Economic Community.
The Dublin and Monaghan bombings kill 34 people.
The Anglo-Irish Agreement gives the Irish government an advisory role in Northern Ireland affairs.
Mary Robinson is elected President of Ireland.
Homosexuality is decriminalised in Ireland.
Ireland votes to allow divorce by referendum.
The Good Friday Agreement is signed.
Ireland adopts the euro as physical currency.
Ireland enters a severe financial and banking crisis.
Ireland votes to allow same-sex marriage.
Ireland votes to repeal the Eighth Amendment on abortion.
The COVID-19 pandemic brings major public health restrictions to Ireland.
